stem: Under the mapping \( w = \dfrac{1}{z} \), find the image of the point \( z = ([[c]]) + ([[d]])i \). Give \( w \) in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
distractor_rationales: This is the conjugate \( \bar z \); inversion also divides by \( |z|^{2} \).
This divides the number itself (not its conjugate) by \( |z|^{2} \), leaving the wrong sign on the imaginary part.
This inverts each component separately, which is not how \( 1/z \) works.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.

stem: Under the mapping \( w = z^{2} \), find the image of the point \( z = ([[c]]) + ([[d]])i \). Give \( w \) in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( |z|^{2} = c^{2}+d^{2} \), not \( z^{2} \); the cross term and sign are both wrong.
This gets the real part right but drops the imaginary cross term entirely.
This squares the conjugate \( \bar z \) instead of \( z \), flipping the sign of the imaginary part.
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by two in the imaginary part.

stem: Under the linear mapping \( w = \left(([[p]]) + ([[q]])i\right)z + \left(([[e]]) + ([[f]])i\right) \), find the image of the point \( z = ([[c]]) + ([[d]])i \). Give \( w \) in rectangular form \( x + yi \).
solution: Substitute \( z=([[c]])+([[d]])i \) into \( w = \left(([[p]])+([[q]])i\right)z + \left(([[e]])+([[f]])i\right) \): first multiply, then add the constant term, giving \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is only the linear (multiplicative) part, forgetting to add the constant term.
This is only the constant term, forgetting to include the multiplicative part.
This adds the coefficient to \( z \) instead of multiplying it by \( z \).
This negates the correct value (a sign slip).
This is off by one in the real part.
This is off by one in the imaginary part.

stem: Under the real linear mapping \( w = [[m]]z + [[k]] \), a fixed point is a value \( z \) with \( w = z \). Find the (real) fixed point of this mapping.
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign in the denominator.
This has the wrong sign in the denominator (added 1 instead of subtracting).
This multiplies instead of dividing when isolating \( z \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
